Title: Identification of a hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA double bond reductase (HDR) affirms multiple pathways for dihydrochalcone formation in apple

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Golden Delicious; Braeburn; Gala; Pinova; Topaz; Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Probable trans-2-enoyl-CoA reductase-like protein; ADH_zinc_N (PF00107); ADH_N GroES-like domain (PF08240)
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondrion for canonical proteoform predicted via N-terminal transit peptide; likely cytosol for N-terminal truncated/alternative-start proteoform
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative pathway via p-coumaroyl-CoA reduction, parallel to naringenin chalcone reduction route
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream; precursor-forming reduction step before phloretin/phloridzin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the C=C double bond of hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters using NADPH, converting p-coumaroyl-CoA to p-dihydrocoumaroyl-CoA; also accepts feruloyl-CoA and sinapoyl-CoA, with only trace activity on caffeoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: p-Coumaroyl-CoA; feruloyl-CoA; sinapoyl-CoA; trace with caffeoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: p-Dihydrocoumaroyl-CoA; likely dihydroferuloyl-CoA and dihydrosinapoyl-CoA; trace dihydrocaffeoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phloridzin; phloretin; in Arabidopsis O-hexosylated p-dihydrocoumaric acid, dihydrocaffeic acid, and dihydroferulic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phloridzin is described as a valuable phytochemical with dietary, pharmaceutical, cosmetic, nutritional and medicinal applications
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple leaves predominantly; also low expression suggests possible relevance in fruits
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor pool for phloretin/phloridzin via p-dihydrocoumaroyl-CoA; in Arabidopsis redirects flux to glycosylated dihydrocinnamic acids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key reaction enzyme for the long-sought reduction step in one phloridzin biosynthetic route; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of reduced hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA products; supports an alternative phloridzin biosynthetic pathway in apple; in Arabidopsis increases O-hexosylated dihydrocinnamic acids but not phloretin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification from apple leaves plus LC-ESI-MS/MS identification; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assays with NADPH and substrate profiling; combined HDR/CHS/CHI assay; stable transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qPCR expression analysis; PacBio Iso-Seq/transcript enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in all tested apple tissues, highest in young leaves and shoot tips, lower in floral organs; Gala lower than Braeburn/Pinova/Topaz; PPGR data indicate expression also in roots and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis transgenic lines, transgene expressed under CaMV 35S promoter; metabolite accumulation strongest in MdHDR-ΔN lines
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; Nos promoter for NptII selection cassette
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young fully developed apple leaves used for enzyme purification; Arabidopsis grown under greenhouse conditions for 4 weeks
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS; CHI (used in coupled assay, not shown as stable interactors)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq long-read transcriptome sequencing and targeted PacBio transcript enrichment; raw data available upon request; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported in Arabidopsis; heterologous expression did not produce phloretin/phloridzin in Arabidopsis, indicating host-metabolism limitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHDR (MD03G1107400) was purified from apple leaves and identified as the enzyme catalysing reduction of p-coumaroyl-CoA. 2. Recombinant MdHDR reduced p-coumaroyl-CoA and some related hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters, but not free acids, p-coumaraldehyde, benzalacetones, or naringenin chalcone. 3. Transgenic Arabidopsis expressing MdHDR proteoforms accumulated O-hexosylated dihydrocinnamic acids, confirming reductase activity in planta. 4. The work supports multiple pathways for dihydrochalcone formation in apple, including a p-coumaroyl-CoA reduction route.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for engineering phloridzin/dihydrochalcone production, especially in combination with downstream pathway genes; truncated/cytosolic proteoform may be more effective for heterologous bioconversion
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Canonical MdHDR contains predicted mitochondrial transit peptide with cleavage around Ala36. No transcript-level evidence for cytosolic splice variant was found, but alternative translation initiation was predicted. N-terminal truncation increased metabolic output in Arabidopsis, suggesting localization-dependent function.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: N-terminal truncated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA double bond reductase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Same as MdHDR core enzyme; lacks N-terminal mitochondrial transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA reduction route linked to dihydrochalcone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses NADPH-dependent reduction of p-coumaroyl-CoA to p-dihydrocoumaroyl-CoA; also active on selected hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: p-Dihydrocoumaroyl-CoA and related reduced CoA esters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: O-hexosylated p-dihydrocoumaric acid; O-hexosylated dihydrocaffeic acid; O-hexosylated dihydroferulic acid; inferred precursor role toward phloretin/phloridzin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential route toward valuable apple dihydrochalcones such as phloridzin with dietary/pharmaceutical interest
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies reduced precursor pool and in Arabidopsis directly leads to accumulation of glycosylated dihydrocinnamic acids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key functional proteoform for effective in planta flux in heterologous system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases glycosylated dihydrocinnamic acids in Arabidopsis, with metabolite levels correlating with transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli with enzyme assays; stable Arabidopsis transformation; qPCR; LC-QTOF metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Engineered proteoform; native transcript not directly demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tested Arabidopsis transgenic lines; line #39 highest
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not yield detectable phloretin or phloridzin in Arabidopsis despite strong metabolite shifts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHDR-ΔN retained catalytic activity in vitro. 2. Arabidopsis lines expressing MdHDR-ΔN accumulated markedly higher levels of O-hexosylated dihydrocinnamic acids than lines expressing canonical MdHDR. 3. Metabolite accumulation correlated strongly with MdHDR-ΔN expression, supporting superior functionality of the likely cytosolic form in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred proteoform for metabolic engineering of reduced hydroxycinnamate flux in heterologous hosts; may be useful in phloridzin bioproduction when stacked with downstream enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents proteoform lacking amino acids 2-35 before predicted cleavage site in one construct; paper also discusses alternative translation initiation generating a longer truncation lacking first 79 aa as another possible cytosolic form. The experimentally main ΔN proteoform outperformed canonical protein in Arabidopsis.
